sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Shortness of breath and swelling in ankles.
History of Present Illness
The patient reports a history of congestive heart failure and diabetes. He states he's been having difficulty controlling his diet and has been experiencing shortness of breath and swelling in his ankles. He denies chest pain. The patient reports weight gain over the past month. He is currently taking insulin and reports avoiding going to insulin. He was previously on diet control and lasix. He is currently taking naproxen and flexeril.
Findings
The patient reports swelling in their ankles. The doctor finds tenderness in the paraspinal areas of the patient's back. Hemoglobin a1c has been running a little bit high. The patient reports difficulty breathing.
Assessment
The patient has congestive heart failure, diabetes, and high blood sugars. The patient is experiencing symptoms consistent with an exacerbation of congestive heart failure.
Plan
The doctor orders an increase in lasix dose to 60 milligrams for the next four days. The doctor orders albuterol as needed. The doctor orders atrovent as needed. The doctor orders physical therapy. The doctor orders naproxen. Follow up in four weeks if symptoms persist.
